examples.title: Cohen-Sutherland Line Clipping Algorithm
examples.description: Complete pseudocode for Cohen-Sutherland line clipping algorithm with outcode calculation and clipping logic
examples.code:
// Cohen-Sutherland Line Clipping Algorithm
// Window: (xmin, ymin) to (xmax, ymax)
// Line: from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2)

CONSTANTS:
 INSIDE = 0 // 0000
 LEFT = 1 // 0001
 RIGHT = 2 // 0010
 BOTTOM = 4 // 0100
 TOP = 8 // 1000

FUNCTION ComputeOutcode(x, y):
 code = INSIDE
 IF x < xmin THEN code = code | LEFT
 IF x > xmax THEN code = code | RIGHT
 IF y < ymin THEN code = code | BOTTOM
 IF y > ymax THEN code = code | TOP
 RETURN code

FUNCTION CohenSutherlandClip(x1, y1, x2, y2):
 // Step 1: Compute outcodes for both endpoints
 code1 = ComputeOutcode(x1, y1)
 code2 = ComputeOutcode(x2, y2)
 
 accept = FALSE
 
 WHILE TRUE DO:
 // Step 2: Trivial acceptance
 IF (code1 OR code2) == INSIDE THEN:
 accept = TRUE
 BREAK
 
 // Step 3: Trivial rejection
 IF (code1 AND code2) != 0 THEN:
 accept = FALSE
 BREAK
 
 // Step 4: Line needs clipping
 // Choose endpoint with non-zero outcode
 IF code1 != 0 THEN code_out = code1
 ELSE code_out = code2
 
 // Step 5: Find intersection point
 IF code_out AND TOP THEN:
 // Clip against top boundary
 x = x1 + (x2 - x1) * (ymax - y1) / (y2 - y1)
 y = ymax
 ELSE IF code_out AND BOTTOM THEN:
 // Clip against bottom boundary
 x = x1 + (x2 - x1) * (ymin - y1) / (y2 - y1)
 y = ymin
 ELSE IF code_out AND RIGHT THEN:
 // Clip against right boundary
 y = y1 + (y2 - y1) * (xmax - x1) / (x2 - x1)
 x = xmax
 ELSE IF code_out AND LEFT THEN:
 // Clip against left boundary
 y = y1 + (y2 - y1) * (xmin - x1) / (x2 - x1)
 x = xmin
 
 // Step 6: Replace appropriate endpoint
 IF code_out == code1 THEN:
 x1 = x
 y1 = y
 code1 = ComputeOutcode(x1, y1)
 ELSE:
 x2 = x
 y2 = y
 code2 = ComputeOutcode(x2, y2)
 
 IF accept THEN:
 RETURN TRUE, (x1, y1), (x2, y2)
 ELSE:
 RETURN FALSE, NULL, NULL
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the complete Cohen-Sutherland algorithm. It computes 4-bit outcodes for endpoints, checks trivial acceptance (both inside) and rejection (both outside), clips against boundaries by computing intersection points, and iterates until acceptance, rejection, or maximum iterations.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only stores endpoints, outcodes, and boundary values

examples.title: Outcode Computation Function
examples.description: Detailed pseudocode for computing the 4-bit region code (outcode) for a point
examples.explanation: This function assigns a 4-bit code where each bit corresponds to a region outside the clipping window. The bits are checked in order: Left, Right, Bottom, Top, and added as powers of 2 (1,2,4,8) to form the final outcode.
